notes: 20 modules — 10 FIT + 10 FIGHT (one pair per stage), dual-path campaign (each stage runs as FIT, FIGHT, or BOTH). Fields: plyo {foot_contact_cap,set_reps,rest_sec,min_recovery_between_plyo_h,amplitude} on plyometric work (NEVER above the beginner cap); sprint_session on sprint work (full recovery); striking_rounds/round_len on fight rounds; medball on throw work. Numbers shown are the NORMAL 'HERO HUNTER' baseline; Easy/Hard scale via campaign.difficulty_scaling. SAFETY (from the report + convention): warm up first incl. shoulders/wrists for striking; plyo 60-100 beginner contacts, 48h moderate / 72h high recovery, no back-to-back plyo; train striking for SPEED not max force (one-punch power is not trainable); wrap hands + gloves for bag work, progressive contact, wrist aligned; contrast training advanced-only (Stage 8). Every module opens with a warm-up and stops on any pain flag (esp. wrist/shoulder) or safety stop criterion. Voice guidance (campaign.voice_guidance) speaks every movement, combination, count, and cue aloud — no reading required.

modules.title: Lean Base + Core
modules.coach_note: Build and preserve the muscle that makes a lean look possible. HONESTY: abs come from whole-body fat loss (diet + energy balance), not ab exercises — spot reduction is a myth. Warm the shoulders before any pressing.

modules.title: First Strikes (Boxing Base)
exercises: Relaxed free round (shadow or light bag)
modules.coach_note: Stance, guard, jab, cross — the alphabet of striking. Relaxed and clean beats hard and sloppy. Wrap hands + gloves for any bag contact; keep the wrist straight. Technique content is conventional coaching (not research-graded).

modules.title: The Explosive Engine (Jump Rope + Plyo)
exercises: Shadowbox flow round (recover the legs, keep the hands moving)
modules.coach_note: Jump rope is the evidence-backed minimal-gear plyo substitute and builds the fast-hands engine. Count contacts, STOP at the cap, land soft. Shadowbox rounds keep it moving while the legs recover. Not on back-to-back days with other plyo.

modules.title: Fast Hands (Boxing Combinations)
modules.coach_note: Speed comes from staying loose — snap and relax, don't muscle it. Voice calls the combos out loud so you never read. Train fast hands, not max force.

modules.title: Explosive Legs + Sprints
exercises: Broad jumps (soft landing, reset each rep)
modules.coach_note: Lower-body force is the real driver of punch power (research) — this is the punch behind the punch. Full recovery between sprints and jumps; quality over volume.

modules.title: The Power Shot (Leg Drive)
exercises: Rear-hand cross — drive from the back foot/hip
modules.coach_note: Power is a whole-body chain from the floor up — not arm strength. Commit, then reset. FANTASY FLAG: a real 'one-punch' knockout isn't a trainable stat; explosive, well-placed shots are. Keep the wrist aligned on contact.

modules.title: Explosive Bodyweight Circuit
modules.coach_note: The explosive bodyweight work that pairs with striking — burpees, tuck jumps, side lunges. Jumps count toward the plyo cap; land soft. Capped output, not AMRAP-to-failure.

modules.title: Dutch Rhythm (Kickboxing)
exercises: Boxing combo into a low kick (e.g. jab–cross–low kick)
Roundhouse low kick (shin, chamber and turn)
Flow round: punches into kicks, one rhythm
modules.coach_note: Dutch style = boxing-forward combos that end on kicks. Turn the hip and land shin-first on the roundhouse; check the wrist on the hands. On a bag, build kick contact gradually. Conventional coaching content.

modules.title: Striker's Shoulders + Dense Arms
exercises: Band pull-aparts + face-pulls (rear delt / rotator cuff)
modules.coach_note: Resilient shoulders let you throw punches for years — rear-delt/cuff and scapular work protect the joint. Dense arms come from progressive rows/chin-ups + push variations. Rotator-cuff protocol is conventional practice, not research-graded here.

modules.title: Centerline (Wing Chun)
exercises: Straight blast — rapid-fire chain down the center
Flow: guard the center, chain the punches
modules.coach_note: Wing Chun's fast straight punches down the centerline — relaxed, rapid, structured. Trapping is drilled solo here (real trapping needs a partner). Presented as traditional/conventional method; evidence for specific WC claims is limited — train it for speed and structure.

modules.title: Ballistic Power (Med-Ball + Upper Plyo)
modules.coach_note: Medicine-ball throws build ballistic upper-body power (a striking proxy, moderate evidence). Throw with intent, full reset. Warm the shoulders thoroughly first; stop on any shoulder pain.

modules.title: Explosive Bursts (Power-Endurance)
modules.coach_note: Power-endurance = keeping bursts fast and sharp as you tire (this IS trainable — unlike max one-punch force). Short explosive bursts, then relax. Voice calls each burst so you never read.

modules.title: The Hunter's Engine (Conditioning)
exercises: Shadowbox rounds between sprints (active recovery, hands moving)
modules.coach_note: Sprint intervals for the fast-twitch engine, shadowbox between for active recovery, light aerobic to round out the gas tank. Sprints keep full recovery; the aerobic block stays easy.

modules.title: High-Output Rounds
exercises: Hold form and speed while breathing hard
modules.coach_note: The gas tank round — keep combinations clean and fast when tired. Quality gate stays on: if form fully breaks, rest, don't grind sloppy.

modules.title: Contrast Training (Advanced)
exercises: Pair A: heavy-ish squat/step-up (3-5 reps) → jump squats (explosive)
Pair B: loaded/hard push (or weighted) → clap/explosive push-ups
modules.coach_note: Contrast/complex = a heavy effort then an explosive one (potentiation). Research supports it for punch speed/force — but it's ADVANCED, unlocked here only. Full rest between pairs; never grind to failure. Load stays moderate.

modules.title: Contrast Striking (Advanced)
exercises: Resisted shadow (band around back) or light-weight shadow — 20s
Immediately: explosive free strikes / bag bursts — 20s
Repeat contrast sets with full rest
modules.coach_note: Loaded/resisted shadow then explosive free strikes — the hands feel lightning-fast after. Advanced only. Light load, full rest, wrist aligned. Never load-strike a bag with weights in hand.

modules.title: Real Evolution (Deload / Recovery)
rules: Deliberately easy — recovery, not a grind
No max plyo, no sprints, no heavy load
modules.coach_note: The honest 'evolution' is recovery + adaptation — this deload makes the finale sharp. Reward completing it; do NOT turn it into a workout. Recovery rests on sleep, food, water.

modules.title: Technical Refinement (Light)
rules: Light and technical — no power, no max output
modules.coach_note: Deload for the hands — refine technique at low intensity so the movement patterns sharpen while the body recovers. No power shots this stage.

modules.title: Hero Hunter — Fit Finale
exercises: Explosive legs: jump squats + broad jumps (capped)
modules.coach_note: The fighter-sprinter engine in one session — explosive, capped, controlled. Enter rested after the deload. Targets, never to failure.

modules.title: Hero Hunter — Every Style
exercises: Round 2: Dutch combo into low kicks
Round 3: Wing Chun centerline chain-punch burst
Round 5: free flow — call your own combinations
modules.coach_note: Every style you learned, one session — boxing, Dutch, Wing Chun, power-endurance, free flow. Fast and clean, capped, never max-force chasing. Voice calls each round and combination. Wrap hands for bag work.
